round2_improvement_notes.linguistic: Agent provided no output in either round; no improvement possible.
round2_improvement_notes.landscape: Acknowledged other agents' findings (ventilation blocks, ornamental plants) to reinforce the initial hypothesis, but did not change the conclusion.
round2_improvement_notes.botanics: Synthesized cross-agent evidence (architectural and regulatory) to strengthen the botanical argument, but maintained the same ranking.
round2_improvement_notes.regulatory: Incorporated meta-agent observations (utility poles, wall patterns) to support the regulatory analysis, reinforcing the initial conclusion.
round2_improvement_notes.meta: Integrated consensus from other agents (botanical profile, road markings) to validate the meta-signatures, maintaining the high confidence.

verdict.judge_synthesis_notes: The judge correctly identified the consensus across all active agents (Landscape, Botany, Regulatory, Meta) pointing to Malaysia. The synthesis explicitly listed the supporting evidence for each domain and correctly noted the absence of evidence from the Linguistic agent. The final decision was well-supported by the convergence of architectural, botanical, and regulatory clues.
